{{Quote|You've the look of a seasoned pazaak player about you. I dabble in it from time to time myself. Care for a game?|translate=1|Sol'aa upon meeting Revan|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
'''Sol'aa''' was an [[Color/Legends|orange]]-skinned, blue-[[Eye/Legends|eyed]] [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] who lived on the [[Farm/Legends|pastoral]] [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]]<ref name="KOTOR" /> in [[3956 BBY]].<ref name="Date">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' places the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', which includes Sol'aa's appearance, in [[3956 BBY]].</ref> They lived in a room located on the north end of the Dantooine [[Jedi Enclave]] and occasionally played the [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]] card game [[Pazaak/Legends|pazaak]]. Sol'aa wore a white [[Clothing/Legends|tunic]], a blue undertunic, beige pants, gray [[Glove/Legends|gloves]], brown [[Boot/Legends|boots]], and a red [[Belt/Legends|belt]]. They understood [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]] but [[Language/Legends|spoke]] in [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]].<ref name="KOTOR" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic}}
While at the Enclave, Sol'aa met the former [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], who was [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] as a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] after [[Amnesia/Legends|losing]] his memories. Sol'aa recognized Revan as a proficient pazaak player and offered a game. When Revan asked them about their identity, Sol'aa was dismissive, hoping to maintain their privacy. Despite the attitude, Sol'aa was still open to playing pazaak with Revan and offered to sell some of their cards. Revan defeated Sol'aa at pazaak five times, and the Twi'lek instead wagered some extra cards as they had run out of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].<ref name="KOTOR" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|The last game was closer than it seemed. Want to try it again?|translate=1|Sol'aa upon defeating Revan at pazaak|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
[[File:GreenSolaa.png|thumb|right|130px|Sol'aa, as he appears in the Xbox version]]
Sol'aa appears as a non-player character in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', developed by [[BioWare]]. If the player attempts to play pazaak without a deck, Sol'aa will provide one. The conversation with Sol'aa is optional and does not progress the plot of the game or change the player's alignment. If the player defeats Sol'aa at pazaak five times in a row, the latter starts wagering cards as opposed to credits. Alternatively, the player could lose to Sol'aa, prompting the Twi'lek to ask if the former wants to try again. Interactions with Sol'aa become unavailable after the player finds four of the five [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Star Map]]s and boards the [[Leviathan (Interdictor-class cruiser)|''Leviathan'']], the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Saul Karath]]. While the player is aboard the ''Leviathan'', the [[Attack on Dantooine (Jedi Civil War)|attack on Dantooine]] commences, and the player is unable to return to the planet.<ref name="KOTOR" />

In the [[Wikipedia:Xbox|Xbox]] version of ''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'', Sol'aa is green-skinned and gray-eyed. Sol'aa wears a brown tunic with white accents, along with orange pants and white boots.<ref name="KOTOR" />
